decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and disruption spells to control the tempo of the game. By playing efficient threats like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent, along with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, the deck aims to pressure opponents while disrupting their game plan. The inclusion of counterspells like Daze and versatile removal like Fatal Push and Snuff Out helps to maintain control of the board. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provide additional card advantage and utility to outmaneuver opponents.

The deck focuses on assembling a combo using Doomsday to win the game. It includes cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Thassa's Oracle to set up the combo, along with support cards like Brainstorm, Thoughtseize, and Force of Will for control and protection. The deck also includes mana acceleration cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to help cast Doomsday efficiently. The goal is to use Doomsday to set up a winning sequence of cards in the library, leading to a victory with Thassa's Oracle.

The deck focuses on using Aluren to combo off and cheat big creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Acererak the Archlich into play for free using cards like Show and Tell and Omniscience. It also includes counterspells like Force of Will and protection like Veil of Summer to ensure the combo goes off smoothly. The deck has a lot of card draw and manipulation to find the pieces it needs, and aims to control the board until it can assemble its powerful combo pieces for victory.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the combo of Doomsday into Thassa's Oracle to win the game. It utilizes a mix of powerful blue spells like Brainstorm and Force of Will to protect the combo, along with fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to help set it up quickly. The deck also includes disruptive creatures like Dauthi Voidwalker and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The key is to carefully plan out the Doomsday pile to ensure a successful win condition.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures and removal spells. By using cards like Brainstorm, Thoughtseize, and Force of Will to control the board and protect key threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent, the deck aims to outmaneuver the opponent and win through aggression and tempo advantage. With a balanced mix of disruption, card selection, and threats, this deck seeks to control the pace of the game and dictate the outcome in its favor.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan with hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Hymn to Tourach, while also having the ability to counter threats with Force of Will and Daze. The deck also utilizes removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to control the board. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student helps draw cards and provide card advantage. The deck also has some evasive threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent to close out the game quickly. The mana base includes fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistent mana production.

This Esper Vial deck focuses on deploying creatures and disruption spells quickly, making use of Aether Vial to cheat out creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck also includes a variety of control elements such as Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Brainstorm to maintain board presence and disrupt opponent strategies. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value with blink effects and card advantage. The deck aims to apply pressure early and control the game through creature recursion and disruption.

This control deck aims to lock down opponents by utilizing lands that disrupt their mana base while also generating value through recursion and powerful land interactions. By constantly recurring lands with Crucible of Worlds and Life from the Loam, the deck can disrupt opponent's strategies with cards like Wasteland and Sphere of Resistance. The deck can also quickly assemble the Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage combo to create a 20/20 Marit Lage token, putting immense pressure on opponents. The deck's strategy revolves around controlling the game through powerful land interactions and disruption cards.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures and powerful spells to quickly overwhelm and disrupt the opponent. By utilizing cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler, Stalactite Stalker, and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to maintain pressure while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Wasteland. The inclusion of cards like Dark Confidant and Mishra's Bauble helps to maintain card advantage and draw into more threats. Overall, the deck's strategy is to be fast, disruptive, and resilient in order to secure a victory.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize the combo of Aluren and Acererak the Archlich to quickly assemble a win condition. By using cards like Show and Tell and Omniscience to get key pieces onto the battlefield, the deck aims to create overwhelming board states that can quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect its combo pieces. By efficiently cycling through the deck with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder, the deck can consistently set up its combo and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of ramp and creature tutors to quickly assemble a powerful board presence, with the ultimate goal of casting Gaea's Cradle to generate a large amount of mana and overwhelm the opponent with big creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth. The deck also features disruption in the form of cards like Collector Ouphe and Wasteland to control the opponent's resources. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through its efficient ramp and versatile toolbox of creatures and spells.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents with counter spells like Force of Will and Daze while also utilizing fast removal like Fatal Push and Snuff Out. The key strategy is to tempo the opponent by deploying threats like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters to apply pressure and control the board. The deck also includes card advantage engines like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brainstorm to maintain resources. With a mix of efficient threats and disruptive spells, the deck aims to quickly close out games while outmaneuvering opponents in the Legacy format.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while deploying efficient threats to close out the game quickly. By combining cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's strategy, alongside Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters to apply pressure, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and secure victory through tempo advantage. Utilizing a mix of removal, card draw, and efficient creatures, this deck is designed to outmaneuver and overwhelm the opponent in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on cheating big, game-winning creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn onto the battlefield quickly using cards like Sneak Attack and Show and Tell. The strategy involves quickly ramping up mana with cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, while also controlling the board with counterspells like Force of Will. A key addition is Atraxa, Grand Unifier, which helps to stabilize the board and protect your combo pieces. The deck aims to win the game in just a few turns by overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures and spells.

The main concept of this UWx Control deck is to control the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage, while win conditions such as Thundertrap Trainer, Flame of Anor, and Murktide Regent provide threats. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student helps dig for important pieces while cards like Brainstorm and Preordain ensure consistency. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, answer threats efficiently, and close out the game with powerful threats. The deck also has a versatile sideboard to adapt to different matchups and strategies.

The main focus of this Dragon Stompy deck is to quickly ramp up mana using Ancient Tomb, Simian Spirit Guide, and Chrome Mox to play disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void and Blood Moon. The deck aims to lock down the opponent's spells with cards like Chalice of the Void, while also utilizing aggressive dragons like Broadside Bombardiers and Pyrogoyf to quickly close out the game. The deck also includes removal spells like Fury and Sundering Eruption to deal with threats on the opponent's side of the board.
